texts.orig: Health Data Systems Veterans Health Administration Department of Veterans Affairs
texts.text: Health Data Systems Veterans Health Administration Department of Veterans Affairs

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This patch introduces the changes necessary to standardize the data stored in the allergy package. Standardized data is necessary for inclusion in the Health Data Repository (HDR). As a result of standardization, sites will no longer be allowed to add or edit entries in either of these files. In addition, users will no longer be able to add “free text” signs /symptoms.
texts.text: This patch introduces the changes necessary to standardize the data stored in the allergy package. Standardized data is necessary for inclusion in the Health Data Repository (HDR). As a result of standardization, sites will no longer be allowed to add or edit entries in either of these files. In addition, users will no longer be able to add “free text” signs /symptoms.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Allergy data will be centrally maintained by Enterprise Terminology Service (ETS).
texts.text: Allergy data will be centrally maintained by Enterprise Terminology Service (ETS).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Allergy data that is standardized will be:
texts.text: Allergy data that is standardized will be:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The following patches are part of the Allergy Domain standardization. They are listed in order of recommended installation:
texts.text: The following patches are part of the Allergy Domain standardization. They are listed in order of recommended installation:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The following is a brief description of all of the patches. For more detailed information, please refer to the patch descriptions or the associated documentation.
texts.text: The following is a brief description of all of the patches. For more detailed information, please refer to the patch descriptions or the associated documentation.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This patch enhances the Master File Server (MFS) mechanism by extending the error reporting with ERR segments in the application acknowledgement. The patch also distributes Allergies parameters.
texts.text: This patch enhances the Master File Server (MFS) mechanism by extending the error reporting with ERR segments in the application acknowledgement. The patch also distributes Allergies parameters.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This patch establishes VUIDs for the Allergies and Pharmacy packages.
texts.text: This patch establishes VUIDs for the Allergies and Pharmacy packages.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This patch adds the VUID data to the following National Drug File files: DRUG INGREDIENTS (#50.416), VA GENERIC (#50.6), VA DRUG CLASS (#50.605), VA PRODUCT (#50.68).
texts.text: This patch adds the VUID data to the following National Drug File files: DRUG INGREDIENTS (#50.416), VA GENERIC (#50.6), VA DRUG CLASS (#50.605), VA PRODUCT (#50.68).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This patch makes changes to the Outpatient Pharmacy VDEF message. Among those changes is the addition of the VUID from the VA PRODUCT (#50.68) File, for the Drug of the prescription.
texts.text: This patch makes changes to the Outpatient Pharmacy VDEF message. Among those changes is the addition of the VUID from the VA PRODUCT (#50.68) File, for the Drug of the prescription.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Reactants may no longer be selected from file 50 in the GUI interface. Sites may now use synonyms when selecting signs/symptoms.
texts.text: Reactants may no longer be selected from file 50 in the GUI interface. Sites may now use synonyms when selecting signs/symptoms.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Locks down and prepares files 120.82 and 120.83 for standardization. Software updated so that only active, standardized terms may be used. Removes ability for site to add local reactants to file 120.82 or to add signs/symptoms to file 120.83. Removes file 50 from the list of files to select a reactant from in the roll and scroll interface.
texts.text: Locks down and prepares files 120.82 and 120.83 for standardization. Software updated so that only active, standardized terms may be used. Removes ability for site to add local reactants to file 120.82 or to add signs/symptoms to file 120.83. Removes file 50 from the list of files to select a reactant from in the roll and scroll interface.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Filters out test patients and patients being merged from having their data sent to the HDR.
texts.text: Filters out test patients and patients being merged from having their data sent to the HDR.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The GMR ALLERGIES file (120.82) and the SIGNS/SYMPTOMS file (120.83) are being standardized. As a result of standardization, sites will no longer be allowed to add or edit entries in either of these files. In addition, users will no longer be able to add "free text" signs /symptoms.
texts.text: The GMR ALLERGIES file (120.82) and the SIGNS/SYMPTOMS file (120.83) are being standardized. As a result of standardization, sites will no longer be allowed to add or edit entries in either of these files. In addition, users will no longer be able to add "free text" signs /symptoms.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The data standardization team has reviewed existing local entries at the sites and has added those terms to files 120.82 and 120.83 as appropriate. Requests for new terms will be made via the New Term Rapid Turnaround (NTRT) process.  If approved, the new term will be sent to all sites for inclusion in file 120.82 or 120.83. For more information on this process, see
texts.text: The data standardization team has reviewed existing local entries at the sites and has added those terms to files 120.82 and 120.83 as appropriate. Requests for new terms will be made via the New Term Rapid Turnaround (NTRT) process.  If approved, the new term will be sent to all sites for inclusion in file 120.82 or 120.83. For more information on this process, see

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In addition to the updates to files 120.82 and 120.83, existing active entries in file 120.8 need to be updated to use standard reactants. In a previous allergy patch, a utility was
texts.text: In addition to the updates to files 120.82 and 120.83, existing active entries in file 120.8 need to be updated to use standard reactants. In a previous allergy patch, a utility was

texts.label: text
texts.orig: distributed that identified free text entries. The utility also included options that allowed the user to fix these entries by either updating them or marking them as entered in error.
texts.text: distributed that identified free text entries. The utility also included options that allowed the user to fix these entries by either updating them or marking them as entered in error.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This patch also introduces two new cross-references and a new Application Programmer Interface (API) that will allow changes to existing reactant terms to propagate through existing allergy entries in the PATIENT ALLERGIES file (120.8).
texts.text: This patch also introduces two new cross-references and a new Application Programmer Interface (API) that will allow changes to existing reactant terms to propagate through existing allergy entries in the PATIENT ALLERGIES file (120.8).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: After standardization patches are installed, VistA files will now contain VUIDs, but not a complete copy of the allergy standard. No changes will be visible in the allergy package until
texts.text: After standardization patches are installed, VistA files will now contain VUIDs, but not a complete copy of the allergy standard. No changes will be visible in the allergy package until

texts.label: text
texts.orig: (ERT) pushes the rest of the standard data and the implementation is flagged as “complete.” The ERT push will occur after hours (after 6 pm local site time). This may happen the same day or several days after installation of the standardization patches.
texts.text: (ERT) pushes the rest of the standard data and the implementation is flagged as “complete.” The ERT push will occur after hours (after 6 pm local site time). This may happen the same day or several days after installation of the standardization patches.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Due to the process of data standardization, there will be additions of new terms to the standard files and inactivation of terms that are not supported in the standard. INACTIVE terms are not selectable for future allergies and Order checks continue to work for INACTIVE terms.
texts.text: Due to the process of data standardization, there will be additions of new terms to the standard files and inactivation of terms that are not supported in the standard. INACTIVE terms are not selectable for future allergies and Order checks continue to work for INACTIVE terms.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The volume of order checks will increase due to standardization
texts.text: The volume of order checks will increase due to standardization

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Standardization includes more terms than most sites currently have.
texts.text: Standardization includes more terms than most sites currently have.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Dietary items such as sea foods and radiology dyes will now contain a drug ingredient of Iodine.
texts.text: Dietary items such as sea foods and radiology dyes will now contain a drug ingredient of Iodine.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Increased order checking supports increased patient safety.
texts.text: Increased order checking supports increased patient safety.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: If a site has altered a nationally released entry by adding drug classes or ingredients or has added a local entry that is now included in the standard, the standardized entry's drug classes and drug ingredients will overwrite any local changes made that are not part of the standard. This may result in decreased order checks, especially if changes were made to accommodate cross-sensitivity.
texts.text: If a site has altered a nationally released entry by adding drug classes or ingredients or has added a local entry that is now included in the standard, the standardized entry's drug classes and drug ingredients will overwrite any local changes made that are not part of the standard. This may result in decreased order checks, especially if changes were made to accommodate cross-sensitivity.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: For terms that are made INACTIVE by the standard, order checking will continue to work from the recorded drug ingredients and drug classes for the item the allergy was recorded against in File 120.82. However, this term will not be available for future selection.
texts.text: For terms that are made INACTIVE by the standard, order checking will continue to work from the recorded drug ingredients and drug classes for the item the allergy was recorded against in File 120.82. However, this term will not be available for future selection.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Existing site files have drug ingredients not included in the standardized file:
texts.text: Existing site files have drug ingredients not included in the standardized file:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Site has Peanut oil in file 120.82 and has 4 Drug ingredients: Peanuts, Peanut oil, IPRATROPIUM and IPRATROPIUM BROMIDE.
texts.text: Site has Peanut oil in file 120.82 and has 4 Drug ingredients: Peanuts, Peanut oil, IPRATROPIUM and IPRATROPIUM BROMIDE.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Standard includes 2 Drug Ingredients: peanut and peanut oil
texts.text: Standard includes 2 Drug Ingredients: peanut and peanut oil

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Order checking after standardization will be triggered by ingredients included in standardized files for Active REACTANTS. There will be two drug ingredients that now trigger order checks.
texts.text: Order checking after standardization will be triggered by ingredients included in standardized files for Active REACTANTS. There will be two drug ingredients that now trigger order checks.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The ingredients listed at the site before standardization will be replaced with the standardized drug ingredients for this REACTANT.
texts.text: The ingredients listed at the site before standardization will be replaced with the standardized drug ingredients for this REACTANT.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Order checks will now be triggered by the standardized drug ingredients.
texts.text: Order checks will now be triggered by the standardized drug ingredients.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Sites may need to change existing clinical reminders,
texts.text: Sites may need to change existing clinical reminders,

texts.label: text
texts.orig: If a clinical reminder was triggered to display based on a recording of an allergy to horse serum, this clinical reminder must be modified now to reflect the change to
texts.text: If a clinical reminder was triggered to display based on a recording of an allergy to horse serum, this clinical reminder must be modified now to reflect the change to

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: We learned from Martinsburg that the more common case is an example of Influenza that was added to File 120.82 at their site. They have a clinical reminder that is being suppressed for any patient that has an allergy to influenza vaccine.
texts.text: We learned from Martinsburg that the more common case is an example of Influenza that was added to File 120.82 at their site. They have a clinical reminder that is being suppressed for any patient that has an allergy to influenza vaccine.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: We are asking sites to assess the impact of standardization of the Allergy files on local clinical reminders.
texts.text: We are asking sites to assess the impact of standardization of the Allergy files on local clinical reminders.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: National clinical reminders have been thoroughly tested in the SQA process and are working as designed.
texts.text: National clinical reminders have been thoroughly tested in the SQA process and are working as designed.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Some synonyms will no longer be available: Examples: ASA, PCN
texts.text: Some synonyms will no longer be available: Examples: ASA, PCN

texts.label: text
texts.orig: With the removal of most drugs from 120.82 and the removal of file 50 from the allergy selection, some synonyms users are accustomed to may no longer be available. The national drug files do not have synonyms. They do have Trade Names, which handles some synonyms, but not the “ASA” for Aspirin or “PCN” for Penicillin type of synonym.
texts.text: With the removal of most drugs from 120.82 and the removal of file 50 from the allergy selection, some synonyms users are accustomed to may no longer be available. The national drug files do not have synonyms. They do have Trade Names, which handles some synonyms, but not the “ASA” for Aspirin or “PCN” for Penicillin type of synonym.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: An NTRT request must be made for additions that sites want to see in the standard.
texts.text: An NTRT request must be made for additions that sites want to see in the standard.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3.1 The Top 10 List for Signs and Symptoms from File 120.83 may now have inactive terms.
texts.text: 3.1 The Top 10 List for Signs and Symptoms from File 120.83 may now have inactive terms.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: mail group will be notified of inactive Top 10 terms at your site after the VETS push has occurred. A new mail message is sent with each push if new entries are inactivated
texts.text: mail group will be notified of inactive Top 10 terms at your site after the VETS push has occurred. A new mail message is sent with each push if new entries are inactivated

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Please replace inactivated terms with standard terms ASAP to prevent clinicians from viewing empty space in the GUI. .
texts.text: Please replace inactivated terms with standard terms ASAP to prevent clinicians from viewing empty space in the GUI. .

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: If the above action is not done, the clinician can click on the scrollbar for the sign and symptom and the empty space will be auto-adjusted and the gap will no longer display.
texts.text: If the above action is not done, the clinician can click on the scrollbar for the sign and symptom and the empty space will be auto-adjusted and the gap will no longer display.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: CPRS GUI v 26 fill fix this so that there is no blank space.
texts.text: CPRS GUI v 26 fill fix this so that there is no blank space.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3.2 Many new synonyms have been added to the ALLERGY REACTANT file #120.82, as part of data standardization.
texts.text: 3.2 Many new synonyms have been added to the ALLERGY REACTANT file #120.82, as part of data standardization.

texts.orig: When you open the Allergy Reactant Lookup window and enter three characters for a causative agent, you might see a list of matches such as this:
texts.text: When you open the Allergy Reactant Lookup window and enter three characters for a causative agent, you might see a list of matches such as this:

texts.text: (The new synonyms are the ones in the <> brackets)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: –Allergy synonyms, if present, are now included in the SIGNS/SYMPTOMS selection box. This is included in patch OR*3*233, which will be distributed with GMRA patch 23. The Signs and Symptoms box in CPRS is populated from File 120.83 REACTIONS or Signs/Symptoms. The synonym for these items in file
texts.text: –Allergy synonyms, if present, are now included in the SIGNS/SYMPTOMS selection box. This is included in patch OR*3*233, which will be distributed with GMRA patch 23. The Signs and Symptoms box in CPRS is populated from File 120.83 REACTIONS or Signs/Symptoms. The synonym for these items in file

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 120.83 will show in the GUI “Enter or Edit Adverse Reaction” box (in < > brackets after the Sign/Symptom.
texts.text: 120.83 will show in the GUI “Enter or Edit Adverse Reaction” box (in < > brackets after the Sign/Symptom.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: You can also search for items in file 120.82 REACTANT by typing in a synonym when entering in an allergy in the GUI. E.g. Tape Adhesive is a synonym of Adhesive Tape.
texts.text: You can also search for items in file 120.82 REACTANT by typing in a synonym when entering in an allergy in the GUI. E.g. Tape Adhesive is a synonym of Adhesive Tape.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Marking Allergies as Entered in Error Now Controlled by Parameter
texts.text: Marking Allergies as Entered in Error Now Controlled by Parameter

texts.label: text
texts.orig: - In CPRS v25, any user could enter new allergies, mark a patient as NKA (no known allergies), and mark allergies entered in error from the cover sheet and the detailed display window. In v.26, the Entered in Error option requires the new parameter OR ALLERGY ENTERED IN ERROR to be enabled for the user. The other options remain open to all users as before.
texts.text: - In CPRS v25, any user could enter new allergies, mark a patient as NKA (no known allergies), and mark allergies entered in error from the cover sheet and the detailed display window. In v.26, the Entered in Error option requires the new parameter OR ALLERGY ENTERED IN ERROR to be enabled for the user. The other options remain open to all users as before.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Free-Text Signs and Symptoms No Longer Allowed
texts.text: Free-Text Signs and Symptoms No Longer Allowed

texts.label: text
texts.orig: – To support data standardization efforts, the ability to enter free-text signs/symptoms was removed. Users must now select items from the list of available signs/symptoms.
texts.text: – To support data standardization efforts, the ability to enter free-text signs/symptoms was removed. Users must now select items from the list of available signs/symptoms.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Inconsistent Sending of Bulletin for Marked on Chart
texts.text: Inconsistent Sending of Bulletin for Marked on Chart

texts.label: text
texts.orig: – CPRS always sent the “Marked on Chart” bulletin if the user entered an allergy from the Orders tab. CPRS never sent the bulletin if the user entered the allergy from the Cover Sheet. This inconsistency has been corrected, and CPRS will never send the bulletin when the user enters a new allergy.
texts.text: – CPRS always sent the “Marked on Chart” bulletin if the user entered an allergy from the Orders tab. CPRS never sent the bulletin if the user entered the allergy from the Cover Sheet. This inconsistency has been corrected, and CPRS will never send the bulletin when the user enters a new allergy.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The “Bulletin has been sent” message that CPRS displays after the user requests the addition of a new causative agent now includes the same warning included in the bulletin about that reactant not being added to the patient’s record.
texts.text: The “Bulletin has been sent” message that CPRS displays after the user requests the addition of a new causative agent now includes the same warning included in the bulletin about that reactant not being added to the patient’s record.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The standard has been sent to sites to enable an assessment of the impact of standardizing files 120.82, and 120.83 on site-added content to these two files. Specifically, sites should compare standardized content with site content for these two files. Then assess the impact standardized content will have on order checks and clinical reminders.
texts.text: The standard has been sent to sites to enable an assessment of the impact of standardizing files 120.82, and 120.83 on site-added content to these two files. Specifically, sites should compare standardized content with site content for these two files. Then assess the impact standardized content will have on order checks and clinical reminders.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Following this assessment, sites should create a mitigation plan to ensure the application of the standard does not disrupt site-developed processes. Content requests will follow the NTRT process after the standard has been implemented.
texts.text: Following this assessment, sites should create a mitigation plan to ensure the application of the standard does not disrupt site-developed processes. Content requests will follow the NTRT process after the standard has been implemented.

texts.orig: 3.6 Request additions to standardized files using the NTRT process
texts.text: 3.6 Request additions to standardized files using the NTRT process

texts.orig: To enter a request go to: REDACTED
texts.text: To enter a request go to: REDACTED

texts.text: Submit research that supports requests for additions, to help expedite the process.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Please wait for contact from your Technical Support Office (TSO) Partner before activating your Allergy and Outpatient Pharmacy VDEF triggers in the VDEF software. They will contact you as soon as they learn from the Data Standardization team that all necessary steps have been completed for your site to be considered fully standardized for Allergy and Outpatient Pharmacy data. Once activated in the VDEF software, your site data will begin transmitting allergy and outpatient pharmacy data to the National HDR.
texts.text: Please wait for contact from your Technical Support Office (TSO) Partner before activating your Allergy and Outpatient Pharmacy VDEF triggers in the VDEF software. They will contact you as soon as they learn from the Data Standardization team that all necessary steps have been completed for your site to be considered fully standardized for Allergy and Outpatient Pharmacy data. Once activated in the VDEF software, your site data will begin transmitting allergy and outpatient pharmacy data to the National HDR.
